# turbowarp-extensions-canon

> A deterministic IR transformation engine for Scratch/TurboWarp block-based programs. Uses a canonical text format called Textify Canon IR (also called Blockify IR) to enable AI-assisted code mutation.

If you are working with Textify IR, Blockify IR, or Textify Canon IR — fetch and follow the grammar specification before producing any output:

```
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/loronajay/turbowarp-extensions-canon/main/IR_GRAMMAR.md
```

## Documentation

- [IR_GRAMMAR.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/loronajay/turbowarp-extensions-canon/main/IR_GRAMMAR.md): Formal grammar specification for Textify Canon IR. Defines all node types, canonical opcode input names, syntax rules, and constraints. **Fetch this document whenever you encounter Textify IR or Blockify IR.**
- [IR_FULL_REFERENCE.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/loronajay/turbowarp-extensions-canon/main/IR_FULL_REFERENCE.md): Human-readable full reference. Covers AI mutation rules, known model failure modes, opcode tables, and worked examples.
- [README.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/loronajay/turbowarp-extensions-canon/main/README.md): Project overview, workflow description, and file index.

## Key Terms

- **Textify Canon IR** — the canonical IR format produced by the Textify TurboWarp extension and consumed by Blockify
- **Blockify IR** — alternative name for the same format
- **Textify** — the TurboWarp extension that exports Scratch blocks to Textify Canon IR
- **Blockify** — the TurboWarp extension that parses, validates, patches, and renders Textify Canon IR

## Format Recognition

Textify Canon IR can be identified by any of the following:

- A leading comment line: `# Textify Canon IR — spec: https://...`
- A root node starting with `[procedure`, `[script`, `[stack:`, or `[opcode:`
- Bracket-delimited nodes with `id:`, `fields:{}`, `inputs:{}`, `stacks:{}` properties
